Question: Unlicensed persons performing real estate activities could be found guilty of a
A: felony and be fined or jailed or both
B: misdemeanor and be fined or jailed or both
C: misdemeanor and be either fined or jailed
D: felony and be jailed for no more than 90 days
Feedback / Explanation: Any unlicensed person who performs any activity that requires a license can be found guilty of a misdemeanor and fined up to $500, sentenced to 6 months in jail, or both

Question: A broker with a minimum of twenty-five (25) years of licensure and an experience-based partial continuing education waiver
A: has no mandatory biennial course requirement to maintain active licensure
B: is required to complete only four hours of elective courses biennially to maintain active licensure
C: is required to complete only the mandatory four-hour core course biennially to maintain active licensure
D: is required to complete six hours of elective courses biennially to maintain active licensure
Feedback / Explanation: A broker with a minimum of 25 years of licensure and an experience-based partial continuing education waiver is required to complete only the mandatory four-hour core course biennially to maintain active licensure

Question: Which of the following statements is most accurate for an applicant who meets all other criteria for licensing?
A: The applicant will be licensed but the license will be placed in “inactive” status until that new licensee can activate under a supervising in-charge licensee
B: The applicant will not be licensed until a supervising in-charge licensee is secured
C: The applicant will be licensed as "pre-active” status until that new licensee can activate under a supervising in-charge licensee
D: The applicant will be licensed as “inactive” status until the Commission appoints a supervising in-charge licensee
Feedback / Explanation: An applicant who meets all other criteria for licensing will be licensed but the license will be placed in “inactive” status until that new licensee can activate under a supervising in-charge licensee

Question: In order to avoid license cancellation in South Carolina, what actions must a resident licensee who becomes a nonresident take?
A: Notify the real  estate commission of the accepting state of active status
B: Notify the real  estate commission of the accepting state of inactive status
C: Notify the Commission in writing, within sixty (60) days of the change in residency and comply with nonresident requirements
D: Notify the Commission in writing, within thirty (30) days of the change in residency and comply with nonresident requirements
Feedback / Explanation: In order to avoid license cancellation in South Carolina, a resident licensee who becomes a nonresident must notify the Commission in writing within thirty (30) days of the change in residency and comply with nonresident requirements

Question: What is the maximum period of time that any licensee may choose to place a license on inactive status?
B: 5 years
C: 8 years
D: Indefinite period of time
Feedback / Explanation: A licensee can place a license on inactive status for an indefinite period of time

Question: Which statement is true about the 2-year renewal cycle for South Carolina licensees?
A: Approximately one half of the licensees must renew in even numbered years and the remainder in odd numbered years
B: All licensees must renew in even numbered years
C: All licensees must renew in odd numbered years
D: All licensees must renew on their initial license application anniversary date
Feedback / Explanation: South Carolina licenses are valid for two years. Approximately one-half of the licensees must renew in even-numbered years and the remainder in odd-numbered years

Question: Which of the following statements is most accurate?
A: A resident licensee may pay a referral fee on a cooperative basis to a brokerage of another state, provided that out-of-state broker is on inactive status in South Carolina
B: A resident licensee may pay a referral fee on a cooperative basis to a brokerage of another state, provided that out-of-state broker does not conduct brokerage services in South Carolina
C: A resident licensee may not pay a referral fee on a cooperative basis to a brokerage of another state
D: A resident licensee may pay a referral fee on a cooperative basis to a brokerage of another state, provided that out-of-state broker had previously held a South Carolina license
Feedback / Explanation: A resident licensee may pay a referral fee on a cooperative basis to a brokerage of another state, provided that out-of-state broker does not conduct brokerage services (including having a place of business) in South Carolina

Question: A nonresident licensee is
A: required to maintain a place of business in South Carolina, if the nonresident expects to receive any compensation or referral fees from transactions conducted within South Carolina
B: required to maintain a place of business in both South Carolina and the state of residency, if the nonresident expects to receive any compensation or referral fees from transactions conducted within South Carolina
C: not required to maintain a place of business in South Carolina, if the nonresident maintains an active place of business in the state of residence
D: not allowed to receive any compensation or referral fees from transactions conducted in South Carolina, unless reciprocity is acknowledge by the both states
Feedback / Explanation: A nonresident licensee is not required to maintain a place of business in South Carolina if the nonresident maintains an active place of business in the state of residence. This is a common practice for towns near the border of the state

Question: Broker James had his license revoked on January 3, 2027. On which of the following dates would his license application be accepted for review?
Feedback / Explanation: After having a license revoked, a person may not reapply for three years from the date of revocation

Question: Applicant Mary successfully completes her exam on December 15, 2027. On which of the following dates will she be unable to apply for a license without first retaking the exam?
Feedback / Explanation: An applicant who passes the examination must apply for a license within one year. After one year, the applicant must reapply for and retake the exam

Question: A property manager-in-charge applicant must have an active property manager license and have completed
A: five (5) hours of instruction in property management accounting and a baccalaureate degree or a master's degree
B: seven (7) hours of instruction in property management accounting and record keeping approved by the Commission
C: ten (10) hours of instruction in property management accounting and record keeping approved by the Commission
D: seven (7) hours of instruction in property management accounting and a baccalaureate degree or a master's degree
Feedback / Explanation: A property manager-in-charge applicant must have an active property manager license and have completed seven (7) hours of instruction in property management accounting and record keeping approved by the Commission

Question: How many South Carolina Real Estate Commissioners are active in the practice of real estate?
Feedback / Explanation: There are 8 South Carolina Real Estate Commission members who are active in the practice of real estate

Question: Once a hearing is conducted, how many days does the Commission have to render a decision and serve notice to a licensee?
B: 60 days
C: 70 days
D: 90 days
Feedback / Explanation: Once a hearing is conducted, the Commission has 90 days to render a decision and serve notice to a licensee

Question: To what entity can a licensee appeal if the Commission issues an adverse order?
B: Court of Appeals
C: First State Courts
D: Real Estate Hearing Review Board
Feedback / Explanation: A licensee can appeal to the Administrative Law Court if the Commission issues an adverse order

Question: Which fund is used to evaluate factors that affect the real estate industry in South Carolina?
A: South Carolina Real Estate Education Program
B: South Carolina Real Estate Commission Education and Research Fund
C: South Carolina Real Estate Business Fund
D: South Carolina Real Estate Development Fund
Feedback / Explanation: The South Carolina Real Estate Commission Education and Research Fund is used to evaluate factors that affect the real estate industry in South Carolina

Question: What type of penalties can be imposed upon an unlicensed person performing real estate activities in South Carolina?
A: Felony and be fined up to $5,000
B: Misdemeanor and be either fined or jailed
C: Felony and be fined or jailed or both
D: Misdemeanor and be fined or jailed or both
Feedback / Explanation: Any unlicensed person who performs any activity that requires a license can be found guilty of a misdemeanor and fined up to $500, sentenced to 6 months in jail, or both

Question: What license status allows a broker to complete only the mandatory four-hour core course biennially to maintain active licensure?
A: A minimum of twenty-two (22) years of licensure
B: A minimum of twenty (20) years of licensure and an experience-based partial continuing education waiver
C: A minimum of twenty-five (25) years of licensure and an experience-based partial continuing education waiver
D: A minimum of twenty-five (25) years of uninterrupted licensure
Feedback / Explanation: If a broker has at least twenty-five years of licensure and an experience-based partial continuing education waiver, then he or she is required to complete only the mandatory four-hour core course biennially to maintain active licensure

Question: An applicant for initial licensure can be licensed, but
A: the license will be placed on “inactive” status until a criminal background search has been completed
B: the license will be placed on “inactive” status until that new licensee can activate under a broker-in-charge
C: will be licensed as "preactive” status until that new licensee can activate under a broker-in-charge
D: will be licensed on “inactive” status until the Commission appoints a broker-in-charge
Feedback / Explanation: An applicant for initial licensure can be licensed, but the license will be placed in “inactive” status until that new licensee can activate under a supervising in-charge licensee

Question: In order to avoid license cancellation in the South Carolina, what actions must a resident licensee take who becomes a nonresident?
A: Notify the Commission of the new state of residence of the licensee's active status in SC
B: Notify the Commission and be placed on inactive status
C: Notify the Commission in writing, within sixty (60) days of the change in residency
D: Notify the Commission in writing, within thirty (30) days of the change in residency and comply with nonresident requirements
Feedback / Explanation: In order to avoid license cancellation in South Carolina, a resident licensee who becomes a nonresident must notify the Commission in writing, within thirty (30) days of the change in residency and comply with nonresident requirements

Question: What is the maximum period of time that any licensee may choose to place his or her license on inactive status?
B: 2 years
C: 5 years
D: 8 years
Feedback / Explanation: There is no maximum period of time that a licensee may choose to place his or her license on inactive status

Question: Which of the following statements is true?
A: All licensees must renew in odd numbered years
B: All licensees must renew in even numbered years
C: Approximately one half of the licensees must renew in even numbered years and the remainder in odd numbered years
D: All licensees must renew on their initial license application anniversary date
Feedback / Explanation: About one half of South Carolina licensees must renew in even numbered years and the other half in odd numbered years

Question: A resident licensee may pay a referral fee on a cooperative basis to a brokerage of another state, provided that the out-of-state broker
A: does not conduct brokerage services in South Carolina
B: is on inactive status in South Carolina
C: previously held a South Carolina real estate license
D: uses a South Carolina-based company for the closing transaction
Feedback / Explanation: A resident licensee may pay a referral fee on a cooperative basis to a brokerage of another state, provided that the out-of-state broker does not conduct brokerage services in South Carolina

Question: Which of the following statements is true?
A: A nonresident licensee is required to maintain a place of business in South Carolina if the nonresident expects to receive any compensation or referral fees from transactions conducted within South Carolina
B: A nonresident licensee can maintain an inactive license in the state of residency, have no active place of business in SC, and receive compensation from transactions conducted within South Carolina
C: A nonresident licensee is required to maintain a place of business in both South Carolina and the state of residency if the nonresident expects to receive any compensation or referral fees from transactions conducted within South Carolina
D: A nonresident licensee is not required to maintain a place of business in South Carolina if the nonresident maintains an active place of business in the state of residence
Feedback / Explanation: A nonresident licensee is not required to maintain a place of business in South Carolina if the nonresident maintains an active place of business in the state of residence

Question: How long must a person wait before reapplying for a license after revocation?
B: 3 years and one day
C: 2 years
D: 90 days
Feedback / Explanation: A person must wait for 3 years before reapplying for a license after revocation

Question: An applicant who passes the examination must apply for a license within what timeframe in order to avoid needing to reapply and retake the exam?
B: One year
C: 90 days
D: Two years
Feedback / Explanation: If an applicant passes the examination, he or she must apply for a license within one year to avoid needing to reapply and retake the exam

Question: A property manager-in-charge applicant must have an active property manager license and must have completed
A: seventeen (17) hours of instruction in property management accounting and record keeping approved by the Commission
B: ten (10) hours of instruction in property management accounting and record keeping approved by the Commission
C: fifteen (15) hours of instruction in property management accounting and a baccalaureate degree or a master's degree
D: seven (7) hours of instruction in property management accounting and record keeping approved by the Commission
Feedback / Explanation: A property manager-in-charge applicant must have an active property manager license and must have completed seven hours of instruction in property management accounting and record keeping approved by the Commission

Question: How many active, licensed real estate commissioners are on the South Carolina Real Estate Commission?
Feedback / Explanation: South Carolina law states that there must be 8 active licensed real estate members on the Commission

Question: Once a hearing is conducted, the Commission has to render a decision and serve notice to a licensee within how many days?
B: 45 days
C: 90 days
D: 120 days
Feedback / Explanation: Once a hearing is conducted, the Commission has to render a decision and serve notice to a licensee within 90 days

Question: What action can a licensee take to appeal an adverse order given by the Commission?
A: Request a case review before the Real Estate Hearing Review Board
B: Request a hearing before the Administrative Law Court
C: Request a hearing before the First State Court
D: Request a case review by the Attorney General's office
Feedback / Explanation: A licensee must request a hearing before the Administrative Law Court to appeal an adverse order given by the Commission

Question: What is the purpose of the South Carolina Real Estate Commission Education and Research Fund?
A: Support a real estate scholarship program for low-income licensee applicants in South Carolina
B: Pay for the publication and distribution of a real estate newsletter in South Carolina
C: Develop real estate training programs
D: Evaluate factors that affect the real estate industry in South Carolina
Feedback / Explanation: The purpose of the South Carolina Real Estate Commission Education and Research Fund is to evaluate factors that affect the real estate industry in South Carolina

Question: A change in residency must be reported to the Commission in writing within
B: 60 days of the change
C: 45 days of the change
D: 90 days of the change
Feedback / Explanation: A change in residency must be reported to the Commission in writing within 30 days of the change

Question: Brokers and salespersons must complete
A: 12 hours of continuing education biennially, 4 of which must be in mandated topics
B: 10 hours of continuing education biennially, 4 of which must be in mandated topics
C: 14 hours of continuing education biennially, 4 of which must be in mandated topics
D: 12 hours of continuing education biennially, 6 of which must be in mandated topics
Feedback / Explanation: Brokers and salespersons must complete 10 hours of continuing education biennially, 4 of which must be in mandated topics

Question: A license is valid for 2 years and expires on
B: 15-Jun
C: 30-Jun
D: 1-May
Feedback / Explanation: A license is valid for 2 years and expires on June 30

Question: Which of the following statements is true?
A: Nonresident licensees are not recognized in SC
B: Nonresident licensees will be recognized in SC only if the licensee’s jurisdiction recognizes SC licensees
C: All nonresident licensees are recognized in SC
D: Nonresident licensees will be recognized in SC after completing 12 hours of approved continuing education courses
Feedback / Explanation: Nonresident licensees will be recognized in SC only if the licensee’s jurisdiction recognizes SC licensees

Question: What is the minimum age to become a property manager in-charge?
B: 19 years old
C: 20 years old
D: 21 years old
Feedback / Explanation: The minimum age to become a property manager in-charge is 21 years old

Question: A branch office must be accessible by the public, investigators, and inspectors
A: during the hours of 9 AM and 3 PM
B: during reasonable business hours
C: during weekend hours
D: at least 25 hours a week
Feedback / Explanation: A branch office must be accessible by the public, investigators, and inspectors during reasonable business hours

Question: Broker-in-charge Dan died on August 1st and associate licensee Sally was assigned to take over Dan's duties. What is the maximum amount to time that Sally can remain in this position?
B: Up to five (5) months
C: Up to 30 days
D: Up to one year
Feedback / Explanation: In the event a broker-in-charge dies, the department may permit an associated licensee to act as broker-in-charge or property manager-in-charge for up to six (6) months

Question: Which of the following statements is most accurate?
A: A broker-in-charge can never be liable for a law violation committed by a supervised licensee
B: A supervised licensee may receive compensation for real estate activities from any person or entity
C: A broker-in-charge may be liable to some degree for law violations committed by a supervised licensee
D: A broker-in-charge must collect all trust funds received in connection with a real estate transaction within 3 business days of the transaction
Feedback / Explanation: Associated licensees are bound by the same laws and regulations that a broker-in-charge is required to follow. A broker-in-charge may be liable to some degree for law violations committed by an associated licensee

Question: Team members
A: are allowed to conduct real estate business from a home office
B: are allowed to conduct real estate business from a car/van
C: must conduct all real estate brokerage activities in a branch office without direct supervision of a broker-in-charge
D: must conduct all real estate brokerage activities from their commission-established office under the supervision of a broker-in-charge
Feedback / Explanation: Team members must conduct all real estate brokerage activities from their commission-established office under the supervision of a broker-in-charge.  They cannot operate only under a salesperson or another broker

Question: A broker-in-charge
A: may not pay a referral fee to an unlicensed individual who is party in the real estate transaction
B: may pay a referral fee to an unlicensed individual who is a party in the real estate transaction
C: may not pay a referral fee to any unlicensed individual
D: may pay an unlicensed employee for those activities the employee performs that require a real estate license
Feedback / Explanation: A broker-in-charge may pay a referral fee to an unlicensed individual who is a party in the real estate transaction under SC law

Question: When must a licensee reveal the license status in a personal real estate transaction?
A: At the earliest practical opportunity after the first substantive contact with a consumer
B: When the customer asks for advice
C: When the customer makes a purchase offer
D: At any time prior to the customer making a purchase offer
Feedback / Explanation: A South Carolina licensee must reveal the license status in a personal real estate transaction at the earliest practical opportunity after first substantive contact with a consumer

Question: Which of the following actions would be classified as "first substantive contact?"
A: When a buyer asks what the asking price is for a property
B: When a customer asks what houses are for sale on the west side of town
C: When a customer agrees to an office meeting
D: When a buyer asks why the owner is selling
Feedback / Explanation: Substantive contact means contact in which a discussion between the consumer and the associated licensee moves from casual introductory talk to a meaningful conversation regarding selling, buying, financial qualifications, or other confidential information that could harm a customer’s bargaining position

Question: Under what conditions must a licensee disclose the nature of a stigmatized property?
A: When a licensee is asked directly regarding a stigma
B: When a death occurred on the property
C: When there are rumors that the property is haunted
D: When the former owner was HIV positive
Feedback / Explanation: A buyer can bring action against an owner or agent of an owner who makes intentional misrepresentations in response to a direct inquiry regarding stigmas associated with real estate

Question: What, if any, action must a licensee take if his client's offer is rejected by a seller without a counter offer being made?
A: The licensee must have the seller sign a commission-promulgated offer rejection form which affirms presentation of the offer
B: The licensee must have the seller and the buyer sign a commission-promulgated offer rejection form which affirms presentation of the offer
C: The licensee must sign a commission-promulgated offer rejection form which affirms presentation of the offer
D: The licensee is required to inform his client within 24 hours of receipt of the rejection notice
Feedback / Explanation: If a client’s offer is rejected by a seller without a counter offer being made, the licensee must sign a commission-promulgated offer rejection form which affirms presentation of the offer

Question: Which of the following statements is most accurate?
A: A broker-in-charge can maintain real estate records only in hardcopy form
B: A broker-in-charge may maintain real estate records electronically as long as a backup copy is stored in an off-site location
C: A broker-in-charge may maintain real estate records electronically as long as a copy is stored in an off-site location in hardcopy form only
D: A broker-in-charge may not use any electronically stored data as back-up copies
Feedback / Explanation: A broker-in-charge may maintain real estate records electronically as long as a backup copy is stored in an off-site location

Question: Which of the following statements best defines the practice of commingling?
A: Depositing escrow money in a business trust account
B: Establishing a business trust account at the institution where a personal account is being held
C: Depositing funds in a business trust account for administrative maintenance purposes
D: Depositing business account trust funds in a licensee’s personal account
Feedback / Explanation: Commingling means mixing or mingling business and personal funds

Question: Which of the following statements best defines the practice of conversion?
A: Adding personal funds to a business trust account to make-up for shortfalls in the balance
B: Using trust funds for a purpose other than the purpose for which they are held
C: Returning trust account funds when a transaction has ended
D: Depositing trust fund monies in an interest-bearing account
Feedback / Explanation: Conversion means using trust funds for a purpose other than the purpose for which they are held. Conversion is a violation of South Carolina real estate license law

Question: Every broker-in-charge must maintain transaction documents for a minimum of
B: 4 years
C: 5 years
D: 7 years
Feedback / Explanation: Every broker-in-charge must maintain transaction documents for a minimum of 5 years

Question: Which of the following account titles would violate South Carolina trust fund laws?
B: Davis Real Estate Office Account
C: Sunshine Real Estate Escrow Account
D: Davis Real Estate Trust Account
Feedback / Explanation: The account must be designated and titled to include the word “trust” or “escrow” in the name of the respective broker-in-charge’s or property manager-in-charge’s licensed real estate firm

Question: Trust funds received by a supervised licensee must be delivered to the supervising licensee
A: no later than the following business day
B: on the same day
C: the next day
D: within two business days
Feedback / Explanation: Trust funds received by an associated licensee must be delivered to the supervising licensee no later than the following business day

Question: How must advance rental property deposits be treated?
B: As Accounts Payable
C: As operating income
D: As trust fund monies
Feedback / Explanation: Advance rental property deposits must be treated as trust fund monies

Question: Which of the following disciplinary actions is within the purview of the Commission in a disciplinary case?
A: Arresting a licensee for serious statute violations
B: Imposing a fine of not less than five hundred ($300) or more than three thousand ($3,000) dollars for each violation
C: Imposing a fine of not more than ten thousand dollars ($10,000) for each violation
D: Serving a disciplinary decision to the charged licensee within 30 days from the effective date of a ruling
Feedback / Explanation: The Commission may impose disciplinary action as a public reprimand, a fine of not less than $500 or more than $5,000, permanently revoke the license, or place a licensee on probation for a definite or indefinite time

Question: What action can be taken by the Commission to stop an unlicensed individual from practicing real estate transactions?
B: Apply to an administrative law judge for a temporary restraining order
C: Issue a "warning letter"
D: Request any state law enforcement entity to seek a bench warrant against the violator
Feedback / Explanation: The Commission can apply to an administrative law judge for a temporary restraining order to stop an unlicensed individual from practicing real estate transactions.  This is done in order to protect the public

Question: Disciplinary investigators must have at least how many hours of real estate training?
B: 80 hours
C: 100 hours
D: 120 hours
Feedback / Explanation: Disciplinary investigators must have at least 100 hours of real estate training

Question: In what section of an Office Policy Manual would you find information regarding office hours, holidays, floor time, sales meetings, dress code, personal safety, workplace privacy, and policies regarding smoking and substance abuse?
B: Employee Classifications
C: Business Procedures
D: Performance Reviews
Feedback / Explanation: In the Office Procedures section of the Office Policy Manual you can find information regarding office hours, holidays, floor time, sales meetings, dress code, personal safety, workplace privacy, and policies regarding smoking and substance abuse

Question: Which of the following is one way trust funds disputes are settled?
B: Voluntary mediation
C: Peer review
D: Commission ruling
Feedback / Explanation: Trust fund disputes can be settled with voluntary mediation

Question: A licensee is legally allowed to distribute escrow funds under which of the following circumstances?
A: At the request of a lien holder
B: To pay for repair damage prior to closing
C: To pay for the recording of a new deed
D: Upon the closing of a transaction
Feedback / Explanation: A licensee is legally allowed to distribute escrow funds upon the closing of a transaction

Question: How long must trust fund monies received in connection with a lease remain in a trust account?
A: Lease transaction trust fund monies can be removed or transferred after six months
B: Lease transaction trust fund monies can be removed or transferred after twelve months
C: Lease transaction trust fund monies must remain in the account until the lease expires or is terminated
D: Lease transaction trust fund monies can be removed if requested by the lessor
Feedback / Explanation: Lease transaction trust fund monies must remain in the account until the lease expires or is terminated.  They must not be converted or commingled with other funds

Question: A trust account must be a
A: demand deposit account located in an insured financial institution authorized to conduct business in South Carolina
B: demand deposit account located in a state-chartered financial institution
C: money market account located in a federally- chartered financial institution
D: savings account located in an insured financial institution authorized to conduct business in South Carolina
Feedback / Explanation: A trust fund account must be a demand deposit account located in an insured financial institution authorized to conduct business in South Carolina

Question: In what period of time must a brokerage inform the Commission of an office telephone number change?
B: Within ten (10) days
C: Within fifteen (15) business days
D: Within five (5) business days
Feedback / Explanation: If there is a telephone number change, a brokerage must inform the Commission within ten days

Question: How many branch offices are allowed under state statutes?
D: Unlimited
Feedback / Explanation: The state statutes allow an unlimited number of branch offices

Question: Which of the following statements best defines a real estate team?
A: Five or more supervised licensees working together as a single unit within a real estate office and supervised by a broker-in-charge
B: Two or more supervised licensees working together, but located in different branch offices
C: Two or more supervised licensees working together as a single unit within a real estate office and supervised by a broker-in-charge
D: Five or more supervised licensees working in a single office under the supervision of a broker-in-charge
Feedback / Explanation: A team is defined as two or more associated licensees working together as a single unit within a real estate office and supervised by a broker-in-charge. Teams have become more popular in brokerage recently

Question: Which of the following team names would violate state statutes?
B: Team Walker Pros
C: Kelli Benson Team
D: Benson Team Professionals
Feedback / Explanation: Team names may not include the terms “realty”, “real estate”, “realtors”, or similar terms suggesting a brokerage

Question: When may a resident licensee pay a part of the commission on a cooperative basis to a person of another state?
A: If that person is not licensed in South Carolina
B: If that brokerage license does not conduct a real estate brokerage service in South Carolina for which compensation is paid
C: Never
D: If that employee does not hold a valid real estate license
Feedback / Explanation: A resident licensee may pay part of the commission on a cooperative basis to a person of another state if that brokerage license does not conduct a real estate brokerage service in South Carolina for which compensation is paid

Question: What, if anything, must a licensee do if wishing to purchase real estate listed with the licensee's own brokerage firm?
A: A licensee must complete form 82-A and file the form with other transaction papers
B: A licensee has no requirements in this situation
C: A licensee must inform all parties of the license status at the time of closing
D: The licensee must make the status as a licensee clearly known in writing to all parties involved
Feedback / Explanation: If a licensee wishes to purchase real estate listed with the affiliated brokerage firm, the broker-in-charge must ensure that the licensee first makes the status as a licensee clearly known in writing to all parties involved

Question: Amelia, a licensee, creates a property description using artificial intelligence.  The program creates a listing that violates the South Carolina Fair Housing Law. Which of the following statements is true?
A: The developers of the technology may be held liable by the South Carolina Human Affairs Commission
B: The seller may be fined
C: The South Carolina Real Estate Commission may lessen Amelia’s fine because the violation was unintentional
D: Amelia alone is responsible for the content of the listing description
Feedback / Explanation: Licensees are solely responsible for work produced by AI, machine learning, or similar programs. Any violations committed through the use of these technologies will be treated as if they were committed directly by the licensee.

Question: License law requires a branch office to be accessible by the public, investigators, and inspectors
A: during the hours of 8 AM and 5 PM
B: whenever the broker-in-charge is on the premises
C: during reasonable business hours
D: at least one weekend day
Feedback / Explanation: License law requires a branch office to be accessible by the public, investigators, and inspectors during reasonable business hours

Question: Licensee Bruce was asked to take over the broker-in-charge duties at his office when broker-in-charge Ruth passed away unexpectedly. Bruce can remain in this position for how long?
B: Up to six (6) months
C: 30 days
D: Up to one year
Feedback / Explanation: In the event a broker-in-charge dies, the department may permit an associated licensee to act as broker-in-charge or property manager-in-charge for up to six (6) months

Question: Which of the following statements is FALSE?
A: A broker-in-charge can never be liable for a law violation committed by an associated licensee
B: An associated licensee may receive compensation for real estate activities only from the employing broker-in-charge
C: A broker-in-charge may be liable to some degree for law violations committed by an associated licensee
D: Trust funds received in connection with a real estate transaction may be deposited into an interest-bearing account
Feedback / Explanation: For the most part, associated licenses are bound by the same laws and regulations that a broker-in-charge or property manager-in-charge is required to follow. A broker-in-charge and a property manager-in-charge may be liable to some degree for law violations committed by an associated licensee

Question: Which of the following statements about team members is true?
A: They may conduct real estate business from a home office
B: They may conduct brokerage activities in a branch office without direct supervision of a broker-in-charge
C: They must conduct all activities from their Commission-established office under the supervision of a broker-in-charge
D: They may conduct real estate business from a car/van
Feedback / Explanation: Team members must conduct all activities from their Commission-established office under the supervision of a broker-in-charge

Question: A broker-in-charge may pay an unlicensed person
A: a percentage of the commission on a real estate transaction
B: a referral fee, as long as that person is a party in the real estate transaction
C: a fee for showing homes to buyers
D: a fee for negotiating an offer for a licensee who is on vacation
Feedback / Explanation: A broker-in-charge may pay an unlicensed person a referral fee, as long as that person is a party in the real estate transaction

Question: When dealing in a real estate transaction where the licensee owns an interest in the property, a licensee must reveal his or her license status
A: when the customer asks for advice
B: when the customer makes a purchase offer
C: at any time prior to the customer making a purchase offer
D: at the first substantive contact with a consumer
Feedback / Explanation: If a licensee owns an interest in the property, a licensee must reveal his or her license status at the first substantive contact with a consumer

Question: Which of the following actions would be classified as "first substantive contact?"
A: When a customer asks what houses are for sale in a particular school district
B: When a customer begins to talk about financial qualifications
C: When a customer comes to the real estate brokerage
D: When a customer calls to get the asking price for a property
Feedback / Explanation: Substantive contact means contact in which a discussion between the consumer and the associated licensee moves from casual introductory talk to a meaningful conversation regarding financial qualifications of the parties

Question: A licensee must disclose the nature of a stigmatized property
A: when there are rumors that the property is haunted
B: when a suicide occurred on the property
C: when a former owner had HIV
D: when asked directly regarding a stigma
Feedback / Explanation: A licensee must disclose the nature of a stigmatized property when asked directly regarding a stigma

Question: When a client's offer is rejected by a seller without a counteroffer being made, the licensee must
A: inform his client within 48 hours of receipt of the rejection notice
B: have the seller and the buyer sign a Commission-promulgated offer rejection form which affirms presentation of the offer
C: sign and deliver a Commission-promulgated offer rejection form within 48 hours
D: have the seller sign a Commission-promulgated offer rejection form which affirms presentation of the offer
Feedback / Explanation: When a client’s offer is rejected by a seller without a counteroffer being made, the licensee must sign a Commission-promulgated offer rejection form which affirms presentation of the offer

Question: Which of the following statements is true?
A: A broker-in-charge must keep all real estate records in hardcopy form only
B: A broker-in-charge may keep electronic records as long as a hardcopy is stored in an off-site location
C: A broker-in-charge may not store electronic data as back-up copies
D: A broker-in-charge may keep real estate records electronically as long as a backup copy is stored in a off-site location
Feedback / Explanation: A broker-in-charge may keep real estate records electronically as long as a backup copy is stored in an off-site location

Question: Commingling can be best described as
A: depositing minimal funds in a trust account to cover bank charges
B: depositing trust funds in a licensee’s personal account
C: establishing a trust account at the same institution where a personal account is held
D: depositing escrow money in a trust account
Feedback / Explanation: Commingling means mixing or mingling; for example, to deposit business account trust funds in the licensee’s personal or general business account

Question: Conversion is defined as
A: depositing trust fund monies in an interest-bearing account
B: using trust funds for a purpose other than the purpose for which they are held
C: returning trust account funds when a transaction has ended
D: adding personal funds to a business trust account to cover shortfalls in the minimum balance
Feedback / Explanation: Conversion means using trust funds for a purpose other than the purpose for which they are held. Conversion is a breach of trust and is a crime as provided by law

Question: For how long must a broker-in-charge maintain transaction documents?
B: 3 years
C: 4 years
D: 5 years
Feedback / Explanation: A broker-in-charge must maintain transaction documents for 5 years

Question: South Carolina would prohibit using which of the following account titles for the deposit of escrow/trust monies?
B: Harris Real Estate Trust Account
C: Becker Real Estate Escrow Account
D: Harris Real Estate Office Account
Feedback / Explanation: The account must be designated and titled to include the word “trust” or the word “escrow” in the name of the respective broker-in-charge’s or property manager-in-charge’s licensed real estate firm

Question: An associated licensee must deliver earnest money deposits to the supervising licensee
B: the next day
C: no later than the following business day
D: within three business days
Feedback / Explanation: An associated licensee must deliver earnest money deposits to the supervising licensee no later than the following business day

Question: Advanced rental property deposits must be treated as
B: trust fund monies
C: accounts payable
D: accounts receivable
Feedback / Explanation: Advanced rental property deposits must be treated as trust fund monies

Question: Which of the following disciplinary actions is available to the Commission for law violations?
B: Issue a public reprimand
C: Arrest a licensee
D: Levy a fine up to $25,000 for each violation
Feedback / Explanation: The Commission may issue a public reprimand for law violations, impose a fine of not less than $500 or more than $5,000, place a licensee on probation, restrict or suspend the individual’s license, or permanently revoke the license

Question: The Commission can stop an unlicensed individual from practicing real estate transactions by
B: issuing a writ of non-compliance
C: requesting a bench warrant against the violator from a law enforcement agency
D: applying to an administrative law judge for a temporary restraining order
Feedback / Explanation: The Commission can stop an unlicensed individual from practicing real estate transactions by applying to an administrative law judge for a temporary restraining order

Question: How many hours of real estate training must a disciplinary investigator have?
B: 100 hours
C: 150 hours
D: 200 hours
Feedback / Explanation: A disciplinary investigator must have 100 hours of real estate training

Question: What is the primary difference between an associate working as an employee versus an associate working as an independent contractor?
A: The independent contractor must receive health coverage from the brokerage while an employee does not typically receive any benefits
B: An employee works under the supervision and control of the broker, but the broker cannot control how the independent contractor associate performs the acts they were hired for
C: An employee has to obey South Carolina license laws while an independent contractor only has to be concerned with national license laws
D: To become an employee, the associate must have at least three years of real estate experience
Feedback / Explanation: A broker-in-charge can hire a salesperson as an employee or an independent contractor. The main difference between the two is an issue of control, as established by income tax laws. An employee works under the supervision and control of the broker. An independent contractor is hired to perform certain acts, but the broker cannot control how the salesperson performs those acts

Question: Trust funds disputes can be settled through
B: peer review
C: Commission ruling
D: involuntary mediation
Feedback / Explanation: Trust funds disputes can be settled through filing an interpleader action

Question: When can a licensee legally distribute escrow funds?
A: When paying for repair damage prior to closing
B: When recording a new deed
C: When the transaction closes
D: When the lender requests it
Feedback / Explanation: A licensee can legally distribute escrow funds when the transaction closes

Question: When dealing with a lease, how long must trust fund monies remain in a trust account?
A: Until the lease expires or is terminated
B: Six months
C: Until the lessor requests release
D: Twelve months
Feedback / Explanation: Trust fund monies must remain in a trust account until the lease expires or is terminated

Question: South Carolina requires a trust account to be located in
B: an insured financial institution authorized to conduct business in South Carolina
C: a Federally-chartered financial institution
D: any insured financial institution, regardless of the state in which is it physically located
Feedback / Explanation: South Carolina requires a trust account to be located in an insured financial institution authorized to conduct business in South Carolina

Question: If a brokerage changes its office name, the broker must inform the Commission of the change
B: within one week
C: within ten days
D: within two weeks
Feedback / Explanation: If a brokerage changes its office name, the broker must inform the Commission of the change within ten days

Question: A broker may have how many branch offices?
Feedback / Explanation: There is no limit to how many branch offices a broker may have

Question: A real estate team is best defined as
A: two or more supervised licensees working together, but located in different branch offices
B: two or more supervised licensees working together as a single unit within a real estate office and supervised by a broker-in-charge
C: three or more supervised licensees working together as a single unit within a real estate office
D: five or more supervised licensees working in a single office under the supervision of a broker-in-charge
Feedback / Explanation: A real estate team is two or more associated licensees working together as a single unit within a real estate office and supervised by a broker-in-charge

Question: Which of the following team names would violate state statutes?
B: Benson Team Professionals
C: Becker Real Estate Team
D: Jim Walker Team
Feedback / Explanation: No team may imply that the team is a separate entity from the employing brokerage firm. Team names may not include the terms "realty", "real estate", "realtors", or similar terms suggesting a brokerage

Question: A resident licensee can pay a part of the commission on a cooperative basis to a person of another state
A: if that person does not hold a valid real estate license
B: as long as that person is not licensed in South Carolina
C: under no circumstance
D: if that brokerage licensee does not conduct a real estate brokerage service in South Carolina for which compensation is paid
Feedback / Explanation: A resident licensee can pay a part of the commission on a cooperative basis to a person of another state if that brokerage license does not conduct a real estate brokerage service in South Carolina for which compensation is paid

Question: Jane wants to purchase real estate listed with the brokerage firm she works for. What must she do?
A: Jane must complete a form specifically designed for this type of situation and keep the form in the transaction file
B: Jane must make her status as a licensee clearly known in writing to all parties involved
C: Jane must inform all parties of her license status at the time of closing
D: Jane has no specific requirement in this situation
Feedback / Explanation: If a licensee wishes to purchase real estate listed with the affiliated brokerage firm, the broker-in-charge must ensure that the licensee first makes the status as a licensee clearly known in writing to all parties involved

Question: If there will be no compensation to other agents, the property
A: cannot be listed in the multiple listing service
B: can be listed in the multiple listing service
C: can be listed as a For Sale By Owner
D: cannot be listed by a licensee
Feedback / Explanation: If there will be no compensation to other agents, the property cannot be listed in the multiple listing service.  This most commonly applies to for-sale-by-owner (FSBO’s)

Question: In what section of a listing contact would you find the agreed upon compensation the broker will receive?
B: Terms
C: Compensation to other agents
D: Multiple listing service
Feedback / Explanation: You can find the agreed upon compensation in the “Terms” section of the listing contract

Question: The mediation clause
A: ends on the closing day of the transaction
B: survives for 60 days after the closing date
C: survives for 90 days after the closing date
D: survives for 120 days after the closing date
Feedback / Explanation: The mediation clause survives for 120 days after the closing date

Question: In what section of the listing contract would you find the agreement to accept electronic signatures?
B: Enforcement
C: Facsimile
D: Signature Block
Feedback / Explanation: You will find the discussion regarding electronic signatures in the “Facsimile” section of the listing contract.  Nearly all real estate transactions allow e-signatures now

Question: What agreement clause requires a buyer to tell a broker about signing an agency agreement with another broker?
B: Indemnification of Broker
C: Broker Notice
D: Broker Liability
Feedback / Explanation: The Indemnification of Broker clause requires a buyer to tell a broker about signing an agency agreement with another broker

Question: Which contract provision explains the limits of the broker’s liability to the buyer with regard to acts of omission, negligence, misrepresentations?
B: Broker Status Limitation
C: Broker Omission Notice
D: Broker Notice of Liability
Feedback / Explanation: The Broker Liability Limitation contract provision explains the limits of the broker’s liability to the buyer with regard to acts of omission, negligence, or misrepresentations

Question: South Carolina does not permit
B: undisclosed dual agency
C: single agency
D: designated agency
Feedback / Explanation: South Carolina does not permit undisclosed dual agency

Question: South Carolina license law requires licensees to present consumers with the Commission-approved
A: South Carolina Broker Agency Listing Agreement
B: South Carolina Agency Listing Options
C: South Carolina Disclosure of Real Estate Transactions
D: South Carolina Disclosure of Real Estate Brokerage Relationships form
Feedback / Explanation: South Carolina license law requires licensees to explain to real estate consumers the information regarding brokerage relationships at the licensee’s firm. Licensees must present consumers with the Commission-approved South Carolina Disclosure of Real Estate Brokerage Relationships form

Question: Which of the following is a “substantive contact”?
A: A customer asks about sales trends
B: A customer wants to talk about finances
C: A customer attends an open house
D: A For Sale by Owner seller places a classified sales ad
Feedback / Explanation: Substantive contact is contact in which a discussion or dialogue between the consumer and the associated licensee moves from casual introductory talk to a meaningful conversation regarding the selling or buying motives or objectives of the seller or buyer, financial qualifications, and other confidential information that if disclosed could harm the consumer’s bargaining position

Question: What action must occur if a licensee has two competing buyer clients in a single real estate transaction?
A: The broker-in-charge must assign a designated agent
B: The broker-in-charge must assign a dual agent
C: The licensee must give written notice to each client that neither will receive confidential information of the other
D: The licensee must select one client, and the other client reverts back to customer status
Feedback / Explanation: If a licensee has two competing buyer clients in a single real estate transaction, the licensee must give written notice to each client that neither will receive confidential information of the other

Question: Informed consent in dual agency is accomplished by
A: each party signing a completed copy of the dual agency agreement required by the Commission
B: each party making an oral statement accepting dual agency
C: the licensee completing Form 56-A, Informed Consent
D: at least one party signing a completed copy of the dual agency agreement required by the Commission
Feedback / Explanation: Informed consent in dual agency is accomplished by each party signing a completed copy of the dual agency agreement required by the Commission

Question: Which of the following statements best defines the action of assigning designated agents?
A: A broker-in-charge assigns a transactional agent to represent  the buyer exclusively
B: A broker-in-charge assigns a transactional agent to represent the seller exclusively
C: A broker-in-charge assigns the same affiliated licensee as a designated agent to represent both clients in the same transaction
D: A broker-in-charge assigns two affiliated licensees as designated agents, each one exclusively representing the different clients in the same transaction
Feedback / Explanation: The broker may specifically designate one or more associated licensees to act as the agent of the buyer or seller to the exclusion of all other associated licensees. Buyers and sellers must give informed consent to enter into designated agency relationships

Question: In a transaction where both buyer and seller are represented by designated agents, the broker-in-charge will act as a
B: designated broker
C: special agent
D: transaction broker
Feedback / Explanation: In a transaction where both buyer and seller are represented by designated agents, the broker-in-charge will act as a dual agent

Question: Which of the following statements about transaction brokerage is most accurate?
A: A transaction brokerage must represent at least one party in a transaction
B: A transaction brokerage may facilitate a transaction without representing either party
C: A transaction brokerage cannot collect compensation from both parties in a transaction
D: A transaction brokerage is not required to disclose customer service duties to a consumer
Feedback / Explanation: A transaction brokerage may facilitate a transaction without representing either party

Question: In what section of a listing contract does the owner agree to comply with South Carolina's withholding tax requirements for nonresident owners?
B: Foreign Investments
C: Taxes
D: Prepaid Fees
Feedback / Explanation: In the “Taxes” section of the listing contract, the owner agrees to comply with South Carolina’s withholding tax requirements for nonresident owners

Question: A property cannot be listed with a multiple listing service if
B: there will be no compensation to other agents
C: the listing agent is a nonresident licensee
D: the commission offered is less than 2% of the sale price
Feedback / Explanation: A property cannot be listed with a multiple listing service if there will be no compensation to other agents

Question: The “Terms” section of a listing contract shows the
B: earnest money amount
C: compensation to other agents
D: compensation the broker will receive
Feedback / Explanation: The “Terms” section of a listing contract shows the compensation the broker will receive

Question: The mediation clause in a listing contract survives for
B: 60 days after the closing date
C: 90 days after the closing date
D: 120 days after the closing date
Feedback / Explanation: The mediation clause in a listing contract survives for 120 days after the closing date

Question: A broker has no duty to disclose what?
B: Lead-based paint information
C: Sex offender information
D: Other offers on a property once one offer has been presented
Feedback / Explanation: A broker has no duty to disclose sex offender information

Question: What agreement clause in a buyer representation contract states that if buyer fails to tell broker about another broker’s involvement, the buyer may be liable to pay broker for any losses or damages resulting from a claim brought against him?
B: Broker Notice
C: Broker Liability
D: Indemnification of Broker
Feedback / Explanation: The Indemnification of Broker clause in a buyer representation contract states that a buyer must tell broker about signing an agency agreement with another broker, or being given information by another broker about a property that is the subject of a contract. If buyer fails to tell broker about another broker’s involvement, buyer may be liable to pay broker for any losses or damages resulting from a claim brought against him

Question: A broker’s liability to the buyer with regard to acts of omission, negligence, misrepresentations is covered in which section of the buyer representation contract?
B: Broker Liability Limitation section
C: Broker Status Limitation section
D: Broker Notice of Liability section
Feedback / Explanation: A broker’s liability to the buyer with regard to acts of omission, negligence, misrepresentations is covered in the Broker Liability Limitation section

Question: South Carolina does not allow the practice of
B: transaction brokerage
C: buyer agency
D: undisclosed dual agency
Feedback / Explanation: South Carolina does not allow the practice of undisclosed dual agency. A real estate brokerage firm may act as a disclosed dual agent only with the prior informed and written consent of all parties. Informed consent is accomplished by each party signing a completed copy of the dual agency agreement required by the Commission

Question: Which form does South Carolina license law require licensees to present to consumers?
A: South Carolina Broker Agency Listing Agreement
B: South Carolina Agency Listing Options
C: South Carolina Disclosure of Real Estate Brokerage Relationships form
D: South Carolina Disclosure of Real Estate Transactions
Feedback / Explanation: South Carolina license law requires licensees to explain to real estate consumers the information regarding brokerage relationships at the licensee’s firm. In order to meet this requirement, licensees must present consumers with the South Carolina Disclosure of Real Estate Brokerage Relationships form to consumers

Question: Substantive contact occurs when a
A: customer talks about why he wants to make a purchase
B: customer asks about sales trends
C: customer attends an open house
D: For-sale-by-owner principal places a classified sales ad
Feedback / Explanation: Substantive contact is a discussion or dialogue between the consumer and the associated licensee moves from casual introductory talk to a meaningful conversation regarding the selling or buying motives or objectives of the seller or buyer, financial qualifications, and other confidential information that if disclosed could harm the consumer’s bargaining position

Question: If a licensee has two competing buyer clients in a single real estate transaction, the
A: broker-in-charge must assign a designated agent
B: broker-in-charge must assign a dual agent
C: licensee must select one client and the other client reverts back to customer status
D: licensee must give written notice to each client that neither will receive confidential information of the other
Feedback / Explanation: If a licensee has two competing buyer clients in a single real estate transaction, the licensee must give written notice to each client that neither will receive confidential information of the other

Question: What action constitutes informed consent in dual agency?
A: Each party making an oral statement accepting dual agency
B: The licensee completing the Informed Consent Form
C: Each party signing a completed copy of the dual agency agreement required by the Commission
D: At least one party signing a completed copy of the dual agency agreement required by the Commission
Feedback / Explanation: Informed consent in dual agency occurs when each party signs a completed copy of the dual agency agreement required by the Commission

Question: Designated agency is the act of the broker-in-charge assigning
A: a transactional agent to represent the buyer exclusively
B: a transactional agent to represent the seller exclusively
C: the same affiliated licensee as a designated agent to represent both clients in the same transaction
D: two affiliated licensees as designated agents, each one exclusively representing the different clients in the same transaction
Feedback / Explanation: A broker-in-charge may assign different affiliated licensees as designated agents to represent exclusively different clients in the same transaction. Buyers and sellers must give informed consent to enter into designated agency relationships

Question: When a buyer and seller are represented by designated agents, the broker-in-charge is a
B: special agent
C: transaction broker
D: dual agent
Feedback / Explanation: When a buyer and seller are represented by designated agents, the broker-in-charge is a dual agent

Question: A transaction broker
A: must represent at least one party in a transaction
B: cannot collect compensation from both parties in a transaction
C: may facilitate a transaction without representing either party
D: is not required to disclose customer service duties to a consumer
Feedback / Explanation: A transaction broker may facilitate a transaction without representing either party

Question: An owner agrees to comply with South Carolina's withholding tax requirements for nonresident owners in the section of the listing agreement called
B: Disclosure
C: Foreign Investments
D: Owner’s Duty
Feedback / Explanation: In the “Taxes” section of the listing agreement, an owner agrees to comply with South Carolina’s withholding tax requirements for nonresident owners

Question: How do buyers and sellers acknowledge that they have read each page of a sales contract?
A: They sign the bottom of the last page of the contract
B: They sign the top of each page of the contract
C: They initial the bottom of each page of the contract
D: They sign the bottom of each page of the contract
Feedback / Explanation: By initialing the bottom of each page of the contract, buyers and sellers acknowledge that they have read each page of a sales contract

Question: What contract addendum takes the place of the repair procedure section of the contract?
B: Property Disclosure Addendum
C: Home Warranty Rider
D: Pre-sale Inspection Report

Question: According to South Carolina law, interest earned on an earnest money deposit belongs to
B: the broker
C: the seller
D: the buyer
Feedback / Explanation: According to South Carolina law, interest earned on an earnest money deposit belongs to the buyer

Question: The mediation clause
B: terminates at the closing
C: extinguishes any mechanic’s lien filing
D: includes any unlawful detainer actions
Feedback / Explanation: The mediation clause survives the closing

Question: South Carolina requires a property condition disclosure for which of these transactions?
B: Installment land sales contract
C: Transfer by foreclosure
D: Sale to a relative
Feedback / Explanation: South Carolina requires a property condition disclosure for the installment land sales contract

Question: Which of the following statements is true regarding the completion of a sales contract?
A: Buyers and sellers sign the bottom of the last page of the contract
B: Buyers and sellers initial the bottom of each page of the contract
C: Sellers initial the bottom of each page of the contract
D: Buyers and sellers sign the bottom of each page of the contract

Question: What is the purpose of a Due Diligence Addendum?
A: The Due Diligence Addendum identifies all the repairs that the seller must complete prior to a closing date
B: The Due Diligence Addendum establishes  a “due diligence period” during which the buyer may terminate the contract with notice
C: The Due Diligence Addendum allows the seller adequate time to make repairs to the property prior to the acceptance of a final offer

Question: According to South Carolina law, interest earned on an earnest money deposit belongs to
B: the broker
C: the seller
D: the buyer
Feedback / Explanation: According to South Carolina law, interest earned on an earnest money deposit belongs to the buyer

Question: Which of the following sales contract clauses survives the closing date?
B: Foreclosure sale clause
C: Mediation clause
D: Option to purchase clause
Feedback / Explanation: The Mediation clause survives the closing date

Question: An installment land sales contract
A: requires a property condition disclosure form to be completed
B: does not require a property condition disclosure form to be completed
C: always requires a land sales tax notice disclosure form to be completed
D: requires a state-approved tax assessment disclosure form to be completed
Feedback / Explanation: An installment land sales contract requires a property condition disclosure form to be completed

Question: The requirement for a property condition disclosure
A: prevents parties from entering into agreements to sell the property “as is.”
B: applies to foreclosure sales
C: does not prevent parties from entering into agreements to sell the property “as is.”
D: applies to transfers by eminent domain
Feedback / Explanation: The requirement for a property condition disclosure does not prevent parties from entering into agreements to sell the property “as is.”

Question: SC law requires that the property condition disclosure statement include
A: the type of material used in the construction of the foundation
B: the replacement of any water heaters
C: only current infestations of wood-destroying insects or organisms
D: current or prior infestations of wood-destroying insects or organisms
Feedback / Explanation: SC law requires that the property condition disclosure statement include current or prior infestations of wood-destroying insects or organisms

Question: Which provision addresses the buyer’s requirement for compliance with South Carolina laws regarding state income tax withholding requirements if the seller is a non-resident or has not filed SC state income tax returns?
A: SC income tax on residential gain
B: SC income tax on a non-resident owner’s gain
C: SC property tax due by foreign investors
D: SC passive gains tax
Feedback / Explanation: According to the provision about SC income tax on a non-resident owner’s gain, the buyer must comply with SC laws regarding state income tax withholding requirements if the seller is a non-resident or has not filled SC state income tax returns

Question: How does a buyer indicate on the sales contract that the transaction is contingent upon the lender’s appraisal?
A: By checking the "Subject to Appraisal" section
B: By checking the "Subject to Financing" section
C: By attaching a lender's letter of approval
D: By signing the "Purchase Price" section
Feedback / Explanation: By checking the “Subject to Financing” section on the sales contract, a buyer indicates that the transaction is contingent upon the lender’s appraisal

Question: Which of the following transactions does not require a special property disclosure form?
A: The sale or exchange of residential property of at least one but not more than four units in an installment land sales transaction
B: The sale or exchange of residential property of at least one but not more than four units
C: The sale or exchange of residential property of at least one but not more than four units in a  lease with an option to purchase transaction
D: The sale or exchange of residential property of at least one but not more than six units in a sales transaction
Feedback / Explanation: South Carolina law requires sellers to disclose any known material defects to potential buyers using a special property condition disclosure form for this purpose. For a residential property of at least one but not more than four units, disclosing the property’s condition is required with the sale or exchange of property, an installment land sales contract, and a lease with an option to purchase

Question: An agent is selecting potential buyers to view a property. What is the most important consideration?
B: The buyer's family size
C: Avoiding any form of discrimination
D: The buyer's preferred closing date
Feedback / Explanation:
In the real estate industry, agents are bound by both ethical considerations and legal requirements when conducting their duties. One of the paramount principles guiding real estate agents is the obligation to treat all parties fairly and without bias. This principle is not only a cornerstone of professional ethics but is also enshrined in laws such as the Fair Housing Act in the United States, which prohibits discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin in the sale, rental, and financing of dwellings.
Avoiding any form of discrimination is the correct consideration for an agent when selecting potential buyers to view a property. This consideration ensures that all individuals have an equal opportunity to purchase or rent properties, regardless of their personal characteristics. It is essential for maintaining the integrity of the real estate market and upholding the rights of all individuals. By prioritizing the avoidance of discrimination, agents not only adhere to legal and ethical standards but also contribute to a more inclusive and equitable real estate environment. This approach fosters trust and respect between agents and clients, which is crucial for the success of any real estate transaction.
The buyer's financial status, while important for ensuring that a transaction can proceed, is not the most important consideration in this context. Financial qualifications are typically assessed later in the buying process, after initial viewings, and must be handled in a manner that still complies with anti-discrimination laws. The buyer's family size could be relevant to the suitability of a property but considering it as a primary factor could inadvertently lead to familial status discrimination, which is prohibited. Lastly, the buyer's preferred closing date is a logistical consideration that, while important for matching buyer and seller timelines, does not supersede the importance of avoiding discrimination in the initial stages of property viewing.
Key Takeaway: The most important consideration for an agent when selecting potential buyers to view a property is avoiding any form of discrimination, ensuring all actions comply with ethical standards and legal requirements.

Question: An agent learns of a property that perfectly matches their client's needs but also suits their own investment criteria. What is the agent's best course of action to adhere to their fiduciary duties?
B: Inform the client about the property first
C: Recommend a less suitable property to the client
D: Wait to see if the client discovers the property
Feedback / Explanation:
In the realm of real estate, agents are bound by fiduciary duties to their clients, which include loyalty, confidentiality, obedience, accountability, and disclosure. These duties mandate that the agent acts in the best interest of their client above all else, including their own interests. When an agent finds a property that not only meets their client's needs but also aligns with their own investment goals, the situation tests the agent's adherence to these fiduciary responsibilities, particularly the duty of loyalty and disclosure.
Informing the client about the property first is the correct course of action because it aligns with the agent's fiduciary duties of loyalty and disclosure. By prioritizing the client's interest and making them aware of a property that perfectly matches their needs, the agent is upholding their obligation to act in the best interest of the client. This action ensures that the client is given the first opportunity to consider and potentially purchase the property, thereby fulfilling the agent's duty to disclose relevant information that could influence the client's decision-making process. This approach not only adheres to ethical standards but also maintains the trust and integrity of the agent-client relationship.
Purchasing the property for themselves is an incorrect action because it directly conflicts with the agent's duty of loyalty to their client. By choosing to act in their own interest before the client's, the agent breaches their fiduciary responsibilities, potentially harming the client's opportunities and interests. This choice could lead to legal repercussions and damage the professional reputation of the agent.
Recommending a less suitable property to the client is also incorrect as it violates the agent's duty to act in the best interest of the client. This action would be considered deceptive and unethical, as it intentionally withholds information about a more suitable property for the sake of the agent's personal gain. Such behavior could lead to a breach of trust and potential legal issues for the agent.
Waiting to see if the client discovers the property is an incorrect approach because it neglects the agent's duty of disclosure. By not proactively informing the client about the property, the agent fails to act in the client's best interest and misses the opportunity to fulfill their fiduciary obligations. This passive approach could result in the client missing out on a potentially ideal property, thereby harming the client-agent relationship.
Key Takeaway: The best course of action for an agent who finds a property that suits both their client's needs and their own investment criteria is to inform the client about the property first, adhering to their fiduciary duties of loyalty and disclosure.

Question: A real estate agent discovers they have a financial interest in a competing property after agreeing to represent a buyer. What should the agent do?
B: Immediately disclose the interest to the buyer
C: Withdraw from representing the buyer
D: Sell the interest in the competing property
Feedback / Explanation:
In the realm of real estate, the principle of agency establishes a fiduciary relationship between an agent and their client, requiring the agent to act in the client's best interest at all times. This includes the duty of full disclosure, which mandates that agents reveal any information that could influence the client's decisions or transactions. A critical aspect of this duty is the disclosure of any potential conflicts of interest, such as a financial interest in a competing property, which could compromise the agent's ability to serve the client impartially.
When a real estate agent discovers they have a financial interest in a competing property after agreeing to represent a buyer, the most ethical and legally sound course of action is to immediately disclose the interest to the buyer. This disclosure ensures transparency and maintains the trust between the agent and the buyer. It allows the buyer to make informed decisions regarding their representation and the properties in question. The disclosure of this conflict of interest is not only a matter of ethical responsibility but also a legal requirement in many jurisdictions to prevent any appearance of impropriety or bias in the transaction process.
Continuing representation without disclosure is an incorrect approach because it violates the principle of full disclosure and can lead to a significant breach of trust between the agent and the buyer. This lack of transparency can have legal repercussions for the agent and potentially damage their professional reputation. Withdrawing from representing the buyer, while seemingly a solution, does not address the underlying issue of the conflict of interest and can leave the buyer without representation at a critical time. This option also fails to uphold the agent's duty to disclose conflicts of interest. Selling the interest in the competing property might mitigate the conflict of interest, but it does not absolve the agent from the responsibility of disclosing the initial conflict to the buyer. Furthermore, the timing and feasibility of selling the interest could complicate the situation further.
Key Takeaway: The most appropriate action for a real estate agent who discovers a financial interest in a competing property is to immediately disclose this conflict of interest to the buyer, ensuring transparency and maintaining the integrity of the agency relationship.

Question: When must a real estate agent disclose their agency relationship to a potential buyer?
A: Before entering into a discussion about the property
B: After the buyer shows interest in making an offer
C: Only if the buyer asks about the relationship
D: At the closing of the property transaction
Feedback / Explanation:
Agency relationships in real estate are foundational to the practice and ethics of the profession. They define the legal and fiduciary responsibilities an agent has to their client, whether they are representing a seller, a buyer, or both in a dual agency situation. One of the most critical aspects of these relationships is the disclosure of the agency relationship to all parties involved. This disclosure is not only a matter of ethical practice but also a legal requirement in many jurisdictions. It ensures transparency in the transaction and helps to manage the expectations and obligations of all parties involved.
Before entering into a discussion about the property is the correct time for a real estate agent to disclose their agency relationship to a potential buyer. This early disclosure is crucial for maintaining transparency and trust in the real estate transaction. It allows the potential buyer to understand whom the agent represents and clarifies the agent's obligations and loyalties before any substantive conversation about the property takes place. This timing helps prevent conflicts of interest and ensures that the buyer can make informed decisions throughout the process. Early disclosure is aligned with the principles of fair dealing and ethical conduct in the real estate profession.
After the buyer shows interest in making an offer, Only if the buyer asks about the relationship, and At the closing of the property transaction are all incorrect because they suggest times that are too late for the initial disclosure of the agency relationship. Disclosing the agency relationship after a buyer shows interest or only if they inquire about it undermines the principle of informed consent and transparency. It could lead to a misunderstanding of the agent's role and potentially compromise the buyer's position in the transaction. Disclosing at the closing of the property transaction is significantly late in the process and fails to provide the buyer with the necessary information to make informed decisions throughout the transaction. Early disclosure, before any detailed discussions about the property, is essential for an ethical and transparent agency relationship in real estate.
Key Takeaway: The agency relationship must be disclosed to a potential buyer before entering into a discussion about the property to ensure transparency, informed consent, and ethical conduct in the real estate transaction.
